Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 1 point with 3 lead changes and 6 ties. The game saw 53 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 100%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q4, 0:02.1, Anunoby (NYK): Anunoby Tip Layup Shot (33 PTS). It swung the win probability by +53.2 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q1, 2:53, Vassell (SAS): Vassell S.FOUL (P1.PN) (Z.Zarba). It swung the win probability by -17.2 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 15, away team 7.
Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.details: Alvarado (NYK) 5 pts, Anunoby (NYK) 5 pts, Brunson (NYK) 5 pts

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q4, 2:21: Brunson 27' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (34 PTS) (Alvarado 3 AST). That single play moved NYK's chance of winning from 18% to 44%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went NYK's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Brunson of NYK. Across 51 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 61 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 10:42, with NYK down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 43%. In other words: very realistic, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q4, 1:47, with the game tilting NYK's way. The play in the middle of it: Anunoby S.FOUL (P2.PN) (Z.Zarba).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
